response:
Short answer
- The 502s were coming from the Application Gateway (appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax) — i.e. a gateway-side failure — not from a WAF CRS regression and not from backend 500 errors.

What we found
- AGW access logs showed the gateway itself returning 502/500 responses (so the origin services were not throwing 500s).
- The investigation also uncovered two independent exposure/egress paths for fazure-mcp / fazure-oauth on the App Gateway (one of the reasons we had to apply per-site policy controls).
- Related work (addressing persistent 403s) showed some trusted VPN IPs had been getting blocked by WAF rules in other cases, but that was separate from the current 502 behavior.

Action/mitigation taken
- Per-site WAF lockdowns and dedicated site WAF policy were created and applied to the App Gateway (scripts/fazure_lockdown.sh and per-site waf scripts) while we continue to trace the AGW-side connection/response issue.
